title: test-matrix.json
description: Consumer scenario list for fs-test-harness. The runner reads `scenarios`; everything else is free-form metadata.

_doc.description: Human note about the matrix's intent.

status.description: Agent bookkeeping. One of `pending`, `claimed-<session>`, `passed-<session>`, `failed-<session>`, `errored-<session>`, `blocked-needs-<feature>`. The runner does not read this; the claim/update/reset scripts mutate it.

image.description: Path under harness.toml [vm.image_dir] to the test image. May be empty for scenarios that build their image at scenario-time.

mount_args.description: Substituted into the mount template's {extra}. Presence of `--rw` flips the scenario into RW mode.

mount.description: Per-scenario mount override. Most scenarios omit this and inherit harness.toml [mount].

ops.description: Ordered ops to execute against the mounted volume. Each op's `type` selects a template from harness.toml [ops]; remaining keys are passed through as substitution tokens and expectation checks.

operations.description: Alias for `ops`, accepted for backwards compat with the existing copies.

_doc.description: Human note about this scenario's intent.

evidence_link.description: Path to the diag dir on success/failure; written by update-scenario-status.sh.

recipe.description: v2 recipe shape: ordered op invocations dispatched by name. Each step's `op` field selects a template declared in harness.toml [ops] (or a built-in like `ship-to-vm` / `ship-to-host`); remaining keys are passed through as substitution tokens. v1 scenarios use `ops` / `operations` instead — the runner accepts either.

op.description: Op name; must match a key in harness.toml [ops] or a built-in (`ship-to-vm`, `ship-to-host`).

type.description: Op kind. Reserved built-in vocabulary: ls, cat, stat, tree, mount, unmount, write, mkdir, rmdir, unlink, rename, cat_via_mount. Consumers may add custom op types with a matching template in harness.toml [ops].

content.description: UTF-8 content for write ops; substituted as {content} for templates.

content_b64.description: Base64 binary content for write ops.

via.description: If "mount", read through the mounted drive instead of the binary's CLI subcommand.
